heading: Extension of more favorable or less favorable treatment than provided under Vienna Convention; authority of President
label: 254c Extension of more favorable or less favorable treatment than provided under Vienna Convention; authority of President

Ex. Ord. No. 12101. Delegation of Functions to Secretary of State Respecting Privileges and Immunities for Diplomatic Missions and PersonnelEx. Ord. No. 12101, , 43 F.R. 54195, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12608, , 52 F.R. 34617, provided:Nov. 17, 1978Sept. 9, 1987By the authority vested in me as President of the United States of America by the Diplomatic Relations Act (, ; et seq.) and of the United States Code, in order to implement the liability insurance and other requirements relating to diplomatic personnel, I hereby designate and empower the Secretary of State to perform, without the approval, ratification, or other action of the President, the functions vested or to be vested in the President by Section 4 of Diplomatic Relations Act (; ).Public Law 95–39392 Stat. 80822 U.S.C. 254aSection 301 of Title 392 Stat. 80922 U.S.C. 254c

Ex. Ord. No. 12101. Delegation of Functions to Secretary of State Respecting Privileges and Immunities for Diplomatic Missions and PersonnelEx. Ord. No. 12101, , 43 F.R. 54195, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12608, , 52 F.R. 34617, provided:Nov. 17, 1978Sept. 9, 1987By the authority vested in me as President of the United States of America by the Diplomatic Relations Act (, ; et seq.) and of the United States Code, in order to implement the liability insurance and other requirements relating to diplomatic personnel, I hereby designate and empower the Secretary of State to perform, without the approval, ratification, or other action of the President, the functions vested or to be vested in the President by Section 4 of Diplomatic Relations Act (; ).Public Law 95–39392 Stat. 80822 U.S.C. 254aSection 301 of Title 392 Stat. 80922 U.S.C. 254c

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President may, on the basis of reciprocity and under such terms and conditions as he may determine, specify privileges and immunities for the mission, the members of the mission, their families, and the diplomatic couriers which result in more favorable treatment or less favorable treatment than is provided under the Vienna Convention.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of State, with the concurrence of the Attorney General, may, on the basis of reciprocity and under such terms and conditions as the Secretary may determine, specify privileges and immunities for a consular post, the members of a consular post, and their families which result in more favorable or less favorable treatment than is provided in the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations, of (T.I.A.S. 6820), entered into force for the United States on .April 24, 1963December 24, 1969

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: Before exercising the authority under paragraph (1), the Secretary of State shall consult with the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate regarding the circumstances that may warrant the need for privileges and immunities providing more favorable or less favorable treatment than is provided in the Vienna Convention.
